sourceMovie.title: The Goonies
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Goonies for the same reason it broke through in 1985: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Richard Donner keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moana
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Goonies clicked for you, Moana (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Time Bandits
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Goonies clicked for you, Time Bandits (1981) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Goonies?
faq.answer: Try Moana, Time Bandits, Muppet Treasure Island—each shares DNA with The Goonies while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Goonies?
faq.answer: The Goonies (1985) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Goonies worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
